descriptions.name: Zwingbat
descriptions.description: A venomous forest serpent feared across the county, it slithers from the treeline to unleash sonic shrieks that terrorize tiny Centims and Millims. It thrives on fear and the eerie silence that follows its retreat back into the woods.

descriptions.name: Centim
descriptions.description: A tiny owl‑like guardian perched along the fences of Old Coplanar, he watches over the plains with a calm vigilance that belies his size. Despite being frequently harassed by Zwingbat’s nightly terror runs, his gentle nature, sharp instincts, and surprising bursts of courage make him a beloved protector of the district.

descriptions.name: Millim
descriptions.description: A spring‑born mischief‑maker from Gag Gulch, he bounds across the Coplanar Plains with endless energy and harmless chaos. Even when Zwingbat sends him tumbling, his quick reflexes and resilient grin keep him ready for the next adventure.

descriptions.name: Trash-Man
descriptions.description: Animated by experimental waste spilled from Coplanar Corp, he stands as Realspace Row’s stubborn, sludge‑born defender. His brute strength, near‑impossible durability, and ability to unleash corrosive muck blasts and rolling stink waves make him a messy but unstoppable force.

descriptions.name: Ball Thing
descriptions.description: A fearsome sphere that bounces across skyscrapers of Realspace Row, its unblinking eyes and menacing maw unsettle everyone who crosses its path. Known for dropping without warning onto unsuspecting pedestrians and revealing rows of razor‑sharp teeth at the worst possible moment, it turns even a calm walk downtown into a nerve‑wracking gamble.

descriptions.name: Abstract
descriptions.identity: Gag Gulch Comic Book Ink Contaminant
descriptions.description: A volatile mass of pure geometry, Abstract twists through Coplanar City with jagged strokes and impossible geometry. Where Line‑Man walks the straight and narrow, Abstract bends every rule of physics, perspective, and common sense. To him, reality is just another page to redraw, erase, or tear apart.

descriptions.name: Line-Man
descriptions.description: A heroic hand-drawn detective who protects Vector Heights, reshaping his linear form into weapons when danger threatens the district. As walking sketch of justice, he cuts through the neon haze with sharp resolve and detective instincts honed on the crooked edges of Coplanar crime.

descriptions.name: Wormy
descriptions.description: A stout worm creature who wriggles through the cartoonish streets of Panel Park, defending its residents with slapstick ferocity and a whip-like tongue.

descriptions.name: Tar
descriptions.description: A tar-born creature who emerged from an old industrial pit on the outskirts of Old Coplanar, now using its fluid form to protect the townsfolk. Its body contains hydrocarbons traced to a meteor fragment that struck the region decades ago.

descriptions.name: Molten-Man
descriptions.description: A towering magma-born menace who erupted from beneath Old Coplanar, some say he is a molten mind fused with broken machinery, but no one can tell whether the lava is driving the tech or the tech is barely containing the lava.

descriptions.name: Bobo the Evil Gingerbread Man
descriptions.description: A deranged gingerbread outlaw baked in the malfunctioning ovens of Gag Gulch, Bobo is the disastrous result of Coplanar Confectionary’s reckless experiments with volatile sweeteners and sinister contraptions.
descriptions.description_2: Tiny but terrifying, he scurries through the valley with frosting‑smeared bravado, a real firearm clutched in one hand and a jittering mechanical arm bolted to the other. Every encounter with Bobo ends in a spray of crumbs, scorch marks, and the unsettling realization that someone at Coplanar Confectionary signed off on this recipe.

descriptions.name: Unknown
descriptions.description: A shifting black haze that moves silently through Pine Hollow, warping the air and disorienting anyone who crosses its path. Locals fear it is the lingering spirit of a long‑buried temple demon, its smoky form searching for a vessel to inhabit.

descriptions.name: Sparky
descriptions.description: Driven half-mad after a bolt of mysterious electrical energy transformed him into living lightning during a high‑school football game, Sparky is obsessed with uncovering what caused the strike. He is convinced someone engineered the strike, and unleashes his raw power on anyone he thinks is hiding the truth.

descriptions.name: Chain
descriptions.description: Chain is a tough‑looking, skateboard‑riding guardian of the city, using his size, swagger, and constant motion to make troublemakers think twice. Though he’s not the most powerful hero around, his presence alone keeps the streets steady—and he’s determined to protect his neighborhood with grit, heart, and a rolling start.

descriptions.name: Sand Monkey
descriptions.description: Sand Monkey is a chaotic beachside menace who stirs up trouble along the shores of Coplanar County, kicking up sandstorms and mischief wherever he scampers. He delights in meddling with tourists, lifeguards, and anyone who dares to relax, turning the coastline into his personal playground of irritation and upheaval.

descriptions.name: Abdominal Snowman
descriptions.description: The Abdominal Snowman is a deranged cartoon snow beast whose entire midsection is literally a giant stomach organ, rumbling and flexing as if it has a mind of its own. It lurks atop the highest peaks of Coplanar County, living for the thrill of devouring anything foolish enough to climb its mountains, turning every ascent into a desperate race against its insatiable hunger.

descriptions.name: Ammo Supplying Robot
descriptions.description: ASR is a compact, cube‑shaped Ammo Supplying Robot, quietly rolling through Coplanar County with no allegiance to heroes or villains. It simply follows its programming with calm precision—dispensing ammunition to whoever requests it—making its neutrality both useful and unnervingly unpredictable.

descriptions.name: Ms. Pac-Man's Evil Ex-Husband From Hell - Bob
descriptions.description: A monstrous, chomping menace whose jagged grin and insatiable appetite make him one of the valley’s most feared cartoon predators, especially if you try to date his ex-wife.

descriptions.name: Grafitti
descriptions.description: A spray-can trickster who streaks through Panel Park, painting living graffiti that crawls across buildings and rewrites the district’s cartoonish skyline.

descriptions.name: David Letter-Man
descriptions.description: He is a walking pile of punchlines and one-liners - literally - since is body is made of jittery, shifting letters. He delivers monologue jokes so sharp even his own vowels wince.

descriptions.name: Invisible Dude
descriptions.description: He's the coolest cat you've never seen. The ladies say he's a real looker... which is impressive, considering none of them can actually see him.

descriptions.name: Mr. Homonym
descriptions.description: A baseball bat with bat wings who can't decide if he's meant to hit things or just flap angrily at them. He screeches puns mid-swing, making him the only weapon that can both smack you and make you laugh at the same time.

descriptions.name: Black Hole Kitty
descriptions.description: A sweet little furball whose face just happens to be a swirling cosmic void that occasionally slurps up loose snacks and unattended shoelaces. She purrs like a collapsing star and somehow makes existential doom feel cuddly.

descriptions.name: Rocket Redd
descriptions.description: Rocket Redd is a wild‑eyed ginger tinkerer who strapped himself to a homemade rocket because “walking is for losers.” He blasts around the valley grinning like a man who trusts his inventions way more than anyone else should.

descriptions.name: Prehistoric Hand
descriptions.description: With the hand’s owner somehow always off‑screen, it casts a gigantic shadow so convincing it sends citizens sprinting for their lives. The shadow causes maximum panic while the hand just kind of… gestures there menacingly.

descriptions.name: Alfred MacKenzy Posh IX
descriptions.description: An entitled aristocrat who treats the entire valley like it’s beneath his polished loafers, and nothing earns his disgust quite like cartoons. He considers them an insult to “proper culture.” No one is aware of just what he has in store...

descriptions.name: Evil Isaac the Tax Collector
descriptions.description: He’s a sharp‑toothed, sharp‑suited rabbit who treats tax collection like a personal protection racket, hopping from door to door with ledgers that look suspiciously like criminal dossiers. Beneath the polite “just doing my job” routine, he runs his own little underworld empire—because in his mind, nothing says power like making everyone terrified of owed carrots.

descriptions.name: Geo Metric
descriptions.description: He’s an angular, malevolent entity built entirely from shifting 3D shapes—cubes, prisms, and razor‑edged polyhedra that click and rotate with unsettling precision. Every edge gleams like a warning sign, and every corner is a deadly hazard.

descriptions.name: The Mime
descriptions.description: A creature of eerie green goo who can briefly mold itself into any human form, using its uncanny mimicry not for deception but for rescue, infiltration, and misdirection. Though its transformations are always a little off, it throws itself into danger with silent, expressive determination.

descriptions.name: CO2
descriptions.description: A calm, sharp‑minded hero who can rip carbon straight out of the air and forge it into striking crystalline structures with a flick of his hand. Despite wielding a power that could level buildings, he uses it with quiet precision, always choosing protection over destruction. In every crisis, he’s the steady presence turning thin air into hope, one crystal at a time.

descriptions.name: Crimson Maker
descriptions.description: A towering killing machine with spiked feet that punch through steel. His lasers cut with cold precision and his flamethrowers flood the field with fire. He moves with brutal purpose and leaves nothing standing.

descriptions.name: Uni-man
descriptions.description: A brilliant scientist who lost his legs in a lab accident and refused to let the world slow him down. He fused himself to a motorized unicycle he controls through a helmet‑mounted cable, zipping into danger with sharp intellect and sharper balance.

descriptions.name: Shattered
descriptions.description: A jagged horror of broken armor and colder intent, Shattered drags himself through the world like a curse that refuses to die. No one knows whether the armor itself lives or if a vengeful spirit puppets the shards from within, but every step he takes grinds like a grave being opened, and every appearance ends with someone begging the darkness for mercy he no longer remembers how to give.

descriptions.name: Silhouette
descriptions.description: A shadowy figure that drifts across Sketchline Fields, flattening into the ground to ambush travelers with sudden, slicing strikes.

descriptions.name: Jake the Ripper
descriptions.description: A masked slasher who stalks the alleys of Realspace Row, striking with surgical precision and vanishing into the shadows before anyone can react.

descriptions.name: Rings
descriptions.description: A floating cluster of metallic rings that drift through the Fractal Timberline, resonating with harmonic tones that bend the forest’s geometry.

descriptions.name: AMPIX
descriptions.description: A crystalline construct from Angle Ranch whose shifting facets store ancient geometric data, making it a living archive of the plains’ 3D anomalies.

descriptions.name: Air-blade
descriptions.description: A sleek geometric warrior forged from aerodynamic shapes, gliding through Vector Heights to slice apart threats with razor-thin air currents.

descriptions.name: Mr. Beep
descriptions.description: A tiny, jittery robot from Gag Gulch who communicates only in comedic beeps and boops, often causing slapstick misunderstandings around the valley.

descriptions.name: Lamprey
descriptions.description: A predatory creature that lurks in the waterways of Pine Hollow, ambushing unsuspecting hikers with its circular rows of teeth.

descriptions.name: Jokes the Giant
descriptions.description: A towering, goofy giant who roams Gag Gulch telling terrible jokes, his booming laughter echoing across the cartoonish valley.

descriptions.name: Scissor Kick
descriptions.description: A hyperactive martial artist from Panel Park who leaps into action with exaggerated, cartoon-perfect scissor kicks that send villains spinning off-screen.

descriptions.name: Cut and Paste Man
descriptions.description: A collage-built villain from Sketchline Fields who slices apart the environment and rearranges it into chaotic new forms.

descriptions.name: Spotlight
descriptions.description: A heroic figure from Sketchline Fields who uses his spotlight to illuminate the darkness and guide others through the district’s dangerous alleys.
